concerns.text: Tasks T015a–T015e introduce 'Scaling Laws,' 'Power-law regression,' and 'membrane network density' metrics. These capabilities are not authorized by spec.md (which limits scope to linear correlation and multivariate regression) nor by plan.md. This constitutes unapproved scope creep and orphan tasks that violate the spec's defined boundaries.

Task T015c requires fetching 'auxiliary data on lipid bilayer composition' from ChEMBL or simulating 'network density.' spec.md defines the dataset strictly as Caco-2 permeability records with SMILES and logPapp; no lipid bilayer data source is defined or authorized. This requirement is impossible to fulfill as written and violates data source constraints.

Task T013b implements a dynamic sampling strategy that drops 'heaviest molecules' to meet RAM constraints. spec.md FR-002 and US-1 require filtering based on data validity (NULL values, protocol heterogeneity), not molecular weight. Arbitrarily dropping heavy molecules introduces selection bias not justified in the spec, violating the requirement for a representative dataset.

Task T006 mandates dynamically generating the 'Computational Method Transparency' section by reading state/...yaml and logs. Constitution Principle VI requires the pipeline to output *scripts* and *raw values* to data/ and code/; a task that only generates a narrative text file does not satisfy the constitutional requirement for version-controlled computational artifacts and reproducibility of the method itself.

Task T019 instructs the model to use 'all computed flexibility descriptors' but plan.md notes full normal-mode analysis is infeasible, approximating via 'internal-coordinate variance.' The task acceptance criteria need clarification on whether 'all descriptors' includes the full NMA suite or just the computed proxies to avoid ambiguity.
